calls.note: Caller is at a 50% loss. A decent company, but it has been underperforming for the last few quarters and there is no revival visible in the results either. For a short-term holder he would say exit when rallies come; for someone in a long-term mode, Hold.

Agreed with the anchor's avoid call. The one positive is a 74% promoter holding, but apart from that everything else looks weak - normally the stock is in their hands. There are better stocks around, so avoid it completely.

Caller Ramesh from Duggirala holds 200 shares bought at Rs 2,650. Fundamentally a good company, and defence is going to come back into the limelight. Hold. For a medium-to-short-term holder, book profit if it comes around the 3,000 level; for the long term, definitely buy on dips and hold. Results are not out yet but the order book is strong, though order execution may slow a bit in this geopolitical environment.

Caller Mallikarjuna from Anantapur has held it since it was RCI Industries. With the defence boom they seem to have simply added the name 'Defence' - even the earnings presentations show only copper and iron rolls. A lot of speculative activity ran and the stock jumped at once; fundamentals are nothing much. Book profit and come out; if the holding is large, definitely book. If it is only 50-100 shares, leave it and treat it as a jackpot ticket.

Caller Srinivasa Rao asked about buying for one to two years. Inox India has recently gone from the 1,000 level to almost 2,000 and is at 1,980. A decent company if he has patience; the stock has risen a lot, so add on dips.
Part of the same one-to-two-year question (name as heard). It is one of three stocks that have all risen a lot, so add on dips rather than chasing here.
Very much an underdog bank, but it has performed well over the last one year - a name discussed at the previous Vijayawada workshop. It has risen a lot along with the other two, so add on dips.
Asked at the current level; results are not out yet. Last quarter was a flattish kind of quarter, and the stock has shot up well from its recent lows. Add slowly somewhere around the 1,500 range.

Caller Sai Mitra wanted to exit SMS Pharma at 380 and switch. Sai Life Sciences is a new kid on the block that is emerging as a good player in CDMO - definitely a company worth investing in, no doubt about that. Buy it on dips and don't ignore it. But he questioned the switch itself: SMS Pharmaceuticals has been a very strong multi-bagger over the last few years, so selling one to buy the other needs a second look.

Asked at current levels for the long term. KEC International is the RPG group; see the results and add on dips. It is a low-beta stock and no big performance is visible over the last few years, so only for someone with a long-term horizon.
